# TESS Program G08046

Title: Planets And Stellar Activity Through Time: Understanding The Evolution, Diversity And Habitability Of Planetary Systems

PI: Battley, Matthew - Queen Mary University Of London

Type: SMALL

Summary: Young exoplanets offer unique insights into the formation and evolution processes that form the diverse population of exoplanets known today. We propose to observe 10,140 young stars with 2-min cadence, in order to search for new young exoplanets and characterise the early evolution of stellar flare activity in order to understand its effects on the diversity and habitability of planetary systems. Our sample is drawn from 55 nearby stellar associations, co-moving groups and open clusters with precise ages from 1Myr 3 Gyr, providing a wide overview of exoplanetary evolution over these early epochs. The longer sectors and adjusted pointing of Cycle 8 will allow us to extend the search to longer-period young exoplanets and better characterise the rotational activity of these young stars.
